coveragePoints.explanation: No infinite autonomous loops, no hiding attempts inside a single agent transcript, no large diffs/logs in workflow state, no treating Temporal Activity retries as semantic reattempts, no automatic replay of every external side effect, no committing failed-attempt memory proposals directly to the repo, no separate recovery systems for resume vs autonomous loops, no full immutable attempt history in the default detail view, no forcing every Step Type into autonomous reattempt semantics.

coveragePoints.title: In-flight Temporal compatibility and versioned cutover
coveragePoints.explanation: New Step Execution and checkpoint contracts that affect Temporal payloads must preserve in-flight compatibility or use an explicit versioned cutover plan; phased rollout (manifests/evidence, checkpoints, gated loops, retrieval/memory, autonomous loop) sequences delivery without breaking running workflows.

coveragePoints.title: Core invariants enforced as workflow guarantees
coveragePoints.explanation: The 17 enumerated invariants are the canonical guarantees of the primitive (stable logical step identity; explicit monotonic run-scoped identity; lineage as optional provenance; new semantic execution creates a new Step Execution; large content in artifacts; append-only evidence; declared source/reason/workspace policy before execution; immutable digest-addressed context bundle; side-effect classification before advance; failed artifacts retained as evidence; accepted-output rule; gate as the only normal publication path; gated external handoff; downstream invalidation/revalidation; resume must not silently degrade to full rerun; MoonMind ownership of identity/checkpoint/evidence/advancement; in-flight payload compatibility).

stories.summary: Step Execution identity and immutable manifest evidence model
stories.description: As a MoonMind workflow author and operator, I need every semantic re-run of a logical step to create a distinct, identifiable Step Execution with an immutable, artifact-backed manifest, so that repeating work is always explicit and each attempt's identity, lineage, reason, status, and disposition are durable and append-only rather than hidden inside agent transcripts or workflow history.
stories.whyItExists: This is the foundational primitive and source-of-truth layering the entire document depends on. Without explicit attempt identity and a manifest contract, no later checkpoint, gate, resume, or invalidation behavior can be made truthful.
stories.scope: The Step Execution primitive and its meaning; the layered source-of-truth authority model and the rule that DB projections are downstream and must not invent attempt truth; run-scoped identity (workflowId, runId, logicalStepId, executionOrdinal) with monotonic ordinals and deterministic identifiers; cross-run lineage as optional provenance with pinned source workflowId/runId; the immutable, append-only manifest contract with bounded reason, status, and terminal-disposition enumerations and compact workflow projection; the core invariant set as canonical guarantees.
stories.outOfScope: Checkpoint capture/validation, workspace/git policy, gating, retrieval/memory bundles, side-effect classification details, resume flow, and operator API surfaces (owned by later stories).
stories.independentTest: Construct two semantic executions of the same logical step and assert distinct monotonic executionOrdinals, deterministic identifiers, an immutable append-only manifest per execution with valid reason/status/disposition values, optional lineage that pins source workflowId/runId without replacing local identity, and a compact workflow-side projection that carries refs only.
acceptanceCriteria: A new semantic execution of a logical step always creates a new Step Execution keyed by (workflowId, runId, logicalStepId, executionOrdinal) with a monotonically increasing ordinal local to the run.
Deterministic stepExecutionId, childWorkflowId, checkpointId, and artifactLinkScope identifiers are derivable from attempt identity.
Manifest records are immutable and append-only; large content stays in artifacts and the workflow carries only a compact projection of refs and bounded summaries.
Reason, status, and terminal disposition values are constrained to the canonical enumerations and reasons are bounded metadata, not free-form transcripts.
Cross-run lineage is optional provenance/display metadata that pins source workflowId and runId and never replaces run-scoped attempt identity.
The documented core invariants are encoded as enforceable guarantees at the workflow boundary.
requirements: Define and enforce run-scoped Step Execution identity and ordinal monotonicity.
Define the immutable manifest artifact contract and its compact workflow projection.
Enumerate canonical reasons, statuses, and terminal dispositions.
Encode lineage as optional pinned provenance separate from durable local identity.
Treat attempt evidence as append-only.

sourceDesignCoverage.ownership: Owns the primitive definition and the layered source-of-truth/authority model including the projection-is-downstream rule.
Owns run-scoped identity, ordinals, and deterministic identifier shapes plus retry vs re-execution vs recover terminology.
Owns the lineage contract (fields, pinned source identity, provenance-only semantics).
Owns the manifest contract and reason/status/terminal-disposition enumerations.
Owns the core invariant set as canonical workflow guarantees; enforcement of individual invariants is distributed to the relevant downstream stories.

risks: Identity/manifest contract changes are Temporal-payload-sensitive; coordinate with STORY-014 for in-flight compatibility.

stories.summary: Step ledger projection and bounded attempt-history operator/API surfaces
stories.description: As an operator, I need the default task view to show the latest attempt per logical step with attempt count, gate status, evidence refs, and preserved/resumed provenance, plus expanded surfaces and bounded APIs to inspect full attempt history, so that I can diagnose repeated work without the system inlining large transcripts, diffs, or provider payloads.
stories.whyItExists: Attempt evidence is only operationally valuable if it is observable. This delivers the read/projection surfaces over the manifest model.

sourceReference.title: Step Executions and Checkpointing
sections: 2 Layering and Source of Truth (step ledger query / DB projection rows)

stories.scope: Default task-detail view showing latest/current attempt, attempt count, blocked/failed gate summaries, latest evidence refs, and preserved/resumed provenance; expanded attempt-history fields; bounded attempt-projection APIs (steps, attempts, single attempt) that return refs and never inline large payloads.
stories.outOfScope: The manifest contract itself (STORY-001) and how individual evidence refs are produced (STORY-004/005/006/007).
stories.independentTest: Given a logical step with multiple recorded executions, the default view returns only the latest attempt plus count and gate summary, the expanded API returns the full bounded attempt list with required fields, and responses contain artifact refs rather than inlined transcripts/diffs/reports.
acceptanceCriteria: Default view shows the latest attempt, attempt count, blocked/failed gate summary, latest evidence refs, and preserved/resumed provenance markers.

Expanded surfaces expose attempt number, lineage, reason, source attempt, runtime child refs, context bundle ref, workspace policy, git disposition, gate verdict, output/diagnostics/diff refs, and terminal disposition.
APIs expose bounded attempt projections and artifact refs and never inline large transcripts, diffs, provider payloads, or verification reports.
The step ledger read projection is derived from workflow state and manifest refs and does not invent attempt truth.
requirements: Provide step ledger latest-attempt projection rows.
Provide bounded attempt-list and single-attempt API responses with required fields.
Enforce ref-only payloads on operator/API surfaces.

sourceDesignCoverage.ownership: Owns operator default/expanded views and bounded attempt-projection APIs.

risks: Projection drift if the read model is not strictly downstream of workflow/manifest truth.

stories.summary: Immutable context bundle with retrieval manifest and memory promotion inputs
stories.description: As a MoonMind workflow, I need each Step Execution to receive an immutable, digest-addressed context bundle that carries compact refs (instructions, prepared inputs, workspace policy/baseline, prior evidence, retrieval manifest, memory manifest) with retrieval recorded as an explicit attempt input and memory side effects tracked with promotion states, so that two attempts that see different context are diagnosable and failed attempts never silently write durable repo memory.
stories.whyItExists: Reliable reattempts and gate diagnosis require that what each attempt saw is explicit, immutable, and reproducible rather than hidden ambient state.

sourceReference.title: Step Executions and Checkpointing
sections: 8 Context Bundle, Retrieval, and Memory Inputs

stories.scope: Context bundle contract (immutable once the execution starts, digest + builder version, compact refs only, no raw credentials, includes workspace policy and baseline); retrieval manifest as an explicit attempt input preserving query/index version/returned refs/filters/excluded refs/prompt summaries; memory promotion states and the rule that failed/abandoned executions must not silently write durable repo memory.
stories.outOfScope: Workspace policy application/checkpointing mechanics (STORY-004/005); gate execution (STORY-007); broad security guardrail enforcement (STORY-012, which references this story's no-credentials rule).
stories.independentTest: Build a context bundle for an execution and assert it is digest-addressed, immutable after start, carries refs not large payloads, records differing retrieval/memory context across two attempts via manifest refs, and that a failed attempt's memory proposal stays in a non-durable promotion state.
acceptanceCriteria: The context bundle is immutable once the Step Execution starts and is digest-addressed with a builder version.
The bundle carries compact refs/summaries and the workspace policy and baseline, never raw credentials or large logs/diffs.
Retrieval is recorded as an explicit attempt input via a retrieval manifest preserving query, index version, returned refs/scores where safe, filters, excluded refs, and included summaries.
Differences in retrieval or memory context between attempts are recorded through context bundle and manifest refs.
Memory side effects carry promotion states and failed/abandoned executions do not write durable repo memory; run-local promotion is allowed only under policy.
requirements: Define and build the immutable digest-addressed context bundle.
Define the retrieval manifest contract as an attempt input.
Define memory promotion states and durable-write gating.

sourceDesignCoverage.ownership: Owns context bundle, retrieval manifest, and memory promotion contracts and the no-durable-write-from-failed-attempts rule.

risks: Context drift if digest/builder version is not enforced; overlap with security guardrails on credential exclusion.

stories.summary: Checkpoint capture, kinds, policy requirements, and validation
stories.description: As a MoonMind workflow, I need durable checkpoints captured at defined boundaries in explicit kinds, with policy-to-checkpoint minimum-evidence requirements and mandatory validation, so that Step Re-executions and failed-step recovery can restore or validate state truthfully and fail explicitly when checkpoint evidence is missing, corrupted, unauthorized, or policy-incompatible.
stories.whyItExists: Checkpoints are the durable recovery evidence that make re-execution and resume truthful; they are a high-risk contract that unlocks workspace policy and resume.
stories.scope: Checkpoint artifact/read-model contract and content type; defined boundaries (after prepare, before mutating execution, after execution, after gates, before publication/external transition, before resume restoration); idempotent checkpoint writes; explicit workspace checkpoint kinds and their restore strength; policy-to-checkpoint minimum-evidence mapping; the validation checklist and the rule that validation failure fails explicitly before launching an agent or mutating the workspace.
stories.outOfScope: Selecting/applying a workspace policy and git-effect classification (STORY-005); the resume orchestration that consumes checkpoints (STORY-009); dependency invalidation (STORY-008).
stories.independentTest: Create checkpoints at each boundary and assert idempotent writes, kind-specific restore strength metadata, and that validation rejects missing/corrupted/unauthorized/plan-mismatched/policy-incompatible checkpoints by failing before any agent launch or workspace mutation.
acceptanceCriteria: Checkpoints are durable artifacts/read-model records with the recommended content type and capture enough state to restore or validate a boundary without parsing logs or UI projections.
Checkpoint evidence is created/updated at the defined boundaries and all checkpoint writes are idempotent.
Each workspace checkpoint kind (git_commit, git_patch, worktree_archive, ephemeral_workspace_ref, external_state_ref) is explicit with its restore strength.
Each workspace policy maps to its minimum required checkpoint evidence and the workflow rejects a policy when required evidence is unavailable.

Validation checks source workflowId/runId, task input snapshot identity, plan identity/digest, logical step identity, attempt provenance, artifact existence/authorization, workspace/branch/commit consistency, kind-policy compatibility, and replay/preserve eligibility, and fails explicitly before launching an agent or mutating the workspace.
requirements: Define the checkpoint artifact contract and boundaries.
Define and enforce idempotent checkpoint writes.
Define checkpoint kinds and restore strength.
Implement the validation checklist with explicit pre-execution failure.

sourceDesignCoverage.ownership: Owns checkpoint boundaries, kinds, policy-to-checkpoint requirements, and the validation contract.

risks: Checkpoint payload shape is Temporal/artifact-sensitive; align with STORY-014; restore strength misclassification could permit unsafe restores.

stories.summary: Workspace and git policy with the accepted-output rule
stories.description: As a MoonMind workflow performing repeated coding work, I need to record an explicit workspace policy before launching a repeated attempt and classify each attempt's git effect, so that a logical implementation step is marked succeeded only when its accepted output is a commit, pushed ref, accepted typed artifact, or explicit no-change disposition, and a dirty uncommitted workspace is never treated as accepted.
stories.whyItExists: Repeated coding work mutates the workspace; safe reattempts require explicit workspace policy, git-effect classification, and a hard accepted-output rule.
stories.scope: Canonical workspace policies (continue_from_previous_execution, restore_pre_execution, apply_previous_execution_diff_to_clean_baseline, start_from_last_passed_commit, fresh_branch_from_source) recorded and visible in attempt metadata/diagnostics; git effect dispositions (accepted/candidate/discarded/superseded/none) with candidate and accepted effect shapes; the accepted-output rule and the prohibition on treating a dirty workspace as accepted; default failed-vs-passing autonomous-loop git behavior.
stories.outOfScope: Capturing/validating the checkpoints these policies require (STORY-004); the gated loop that selects when to reattempt (STORY-007); runtime/session policy (STORY-010).
stories.independentTest: Apply each workspace policy with its required checkpoint present and assert the policy is recorded in metadata, the git effect is classified correctly, a passing attempt that commits is marked accepted, and a failed attempt with a dirty tree is recorded as candidate/discarded but never accepted.
acceptanceCriteria: A workspace policy is recorded before launching a repeated attempt and is visible in Step Execution metadata and operator diagnostics.
Every mutating Step Execution classifies its git effect using the defined dispositions.

A logical implementation step is marked succeeded only when its accepted output is a commit SHA, a pushed branch/ref, an accepted typed non-git artifact, or an explicit accepted no-change disposition.
A dirty uncommitted workspace is never treated as an accepted state.

For autonomous coding/story loops the default is: failed/partial attempt yields diff artifact only with no advancement; passing attempt commits, records an accepted git effect, and advances the logical step.
requirements: Define and record canonical workspace policies.
Classify git effects per mutating attempt.
Enforce the accepted-output rule and reject dirty-as-accepted.

sourceDesignCoverage.ownership: Owns workspace policies, git effect states, and the accepted-output rule.

risks: Policies depend on checkpoint availability from STORY-004; incorrect acceptance could publish unverified work.

stories.summary: Side-effect classification, idempotency keys, and external handoff guardrails
stories.description: As a MoonMind workflow, I need every nontrivial attempt to classify its side effects before advancing and to guard side-effecting activities with deterministic idempotency keys, so that non-idempotent external work is never hidden inside repeated implementation attempts and publication, Jira, merge, deploy, and provider-account actions only run from gate-approved state.
stories.whyItExists: Reattempts are unsafe unless external and local side effects are explicitly classified and guarded; this protects external systems and billing-relevant operations.
stories.scope: Side-effect classes (workspace_mutation, artifact_write, external_idempotent, external_non_idempotent, publication, provider_account, memory_update, retrieval_index_update) with reattempt posture; classification recorded before the workflow advances; deterministic idempotency-key derivation for side-effecting activities and the rule that activity attempt numbers are not the primary business key; explicit, idempotent, observable cleanup/compensation; accounting for already-occurred non-idempotent effects.
stories.outOfScope: The gate machinery that approves publication (STORY-007); broad security posture such as credential redaction and path-traversal prevention (STORY-012); git effect dispositions (STORY-005).
stories.independentTest: Submit attempts producing each side-effect class and assert classification is recorded before advancement, idempotency keys are derived from attempt identity and stable across retries, non-idempotent external work is blocked in autonomous reattempts absent explicit policy, and publication-class effects require gate-approved state.
acceptanceCriteria: Every nontrivial attempt classifies its side effects before the workflow advances.
All side-effecting activities accept or derive stable idempotency keys with the default key shape; activity attempt numbers are never the primary business idempotency key.
Non-idempotent external work is not hidden inside repeated implementation attempts and is forbidden in autonomous reattempts unless explicit policy permits.
Publication, Jira transitions, merge, deployment, and provider-account actions require gate-approved workflow state.
Already-occurred non-idempotent effects are accounted for explicitly and cleanup/compensation is explicit, idempotent, and observable.
requirements: Define side-effect classes and reattempt posture.
Derive deterministic idempotency keys from attempt identity.
Gate external/publication side effects on approved state.
Define explicit idempotent observable compensation.

sourceDesignCoverage.ownership: Owns deterministic idempotency-key derivation for side-effecting activities.
Owns side-effect classification and external/publication guardrails.

risks: Compatibility-sensitive: idempotency keys and gating must not change billing-relevant semantics; coordinate with STORY-007 for gate-approved publication.

stories.summary: Workflow-owned gated iteration with structured verdicts, budgets, and stop semantics
stories.description: As a MoonMind operator, I need the parent workflow to own repetition (implement -> verify -> remediate -> verify) by branching on structured gate verdicts under explicit budgets, and to classify a deterministic terminal disposition when budgets are exhausted, so that autonomous loops advance to publication only on a passing gate and otherwise stop with published evidence and a recommended next action.
stories.whyItExists: Gated iteration is the mechanism that makes repeated work safe and bounded; the gate is the only normal path from repeated implementation work to publication.
stories.scope: Workflow-owned repetition policy where the parent workflow owns whether another attempt is allowed, which step/group repeats, verdict handling, downstream skip/invalidate on gate failure, and final publication eligibility; the structured gate verdict contract (verdict/confidence/validated/invalidated refs/recommended next action) with branching on structured results rather than prose; explicit budget dimensions and deterministic terminal dispositions on exhaustion with published latest evidence; failure/stop terminal-disposition classification and downstream gating on failed gates.
stories.outOfScope: Producing the workspace state a gate validates (STORY-005); side-effect/publication guardrails the gate authorizes (STORY-006); dependency invalidation mechanics across steps (STORY-008, referenced here for downstream handling).
stories.independentTest: Run a verify/remediate loop where the gate returns ADDITIONAL_WORK_NEEDED until budget exhaustion and assert the workflow branches only on structured verdicts, stops before publication with a deterministic terminal disposition and published evidence, and that a FULLY_IMPLEMENTED verdict is required before publication; also assert a failed gate causes the dependent downstream step to be skipped/blocked/invalidated/revalidated.
acceptanceCriteria: The gated iteration loop is owned by the parent workflow, not only by agent instructions, including attempt allowance, which step repeats, verdict pass/retry/fail/attention handling, downstream skip/invalidate, and publication eligibility.
Gate-producing steps return structured verdicts and the workflow branches on the structured result, never on parsed prose.

Every autonomous loop has explicit budgets and stops with a deterministic terminal disposition (needs_human/blocked/failed_with_remaining_work) on exhaustion, publishing latest evidence and a recommended next action.
A passing gate (FULLY_IMPLEMENTED) is required before publication/external handoff proceeds.
When a downstream step depends on a passing gate, the workflow skips/blocks/invalidates/revalidates it on gate failure and publication never relies only on the downstream agent noticing the failure.
requirements: Implement workflow-owned gated repetition.
Define the structured gate verdict contract.
Define budgets, stop rules, and terminal dispositions.
Gate publication on passing verdicts and handle downstream steps on failure.

sourceDesignCoverage.ownership: Owns the gated iteration loop, verdict contract, and budgets/stop rules.
Owns failure/stop terminal-disposition classification and downstream gating on failed gates.

risks: Loop ownership must stay in the workflow; budget exhaustion must be deterministic to avoid runaway loops (non-goal).

stories.summary: Dependency invalidation and preserved output reuse
stories.description: As a MoonMind workflow, I need outputs bound to the producing attempt identity so that when a repeated attempt changes an accepted upstream output, downstream steps that consumed the old output are invalidated or revalidated before reuse, so the system never presents downstream work as valid when it was built against a superseded upstream attempt.
stories.whyItExists: Both failed-step recovery and autonomous loops can change accepted outputs; without invalidation, downstream work can be silently stale.

sourceReference.title: Step Executions and Checkpointing
sections: 14 Dependency Invalidation and Preserved Output Reuse

stories.scope: Binding outputs to producing attempt identity; downstream inputs resolving to a specific producing execution output rather than ambiguous latest; marking dependents pending/blocked/superseded/requires_revalidation when accepted outputs change; preservedFrom identity on preserved steps and reuse only when a checkpoint proves contract satisfaction; structured (not prose) revalidation evidence.
stories.outOfScope: Checkpoint capture/validation primitives (STORY-004); the resume flow that imports preserved steps (STORY-009); gate verdict mechanics (STORY-007).
stories.independentTest: Change an accepted upstream output to a new version and assert downstream steps that consumed the old version are marked for invalidation/revalidation, preserved outputs are reused only when a checkpoint proves they still satisfy downstream contracts, and revalidation results are structured evidence.
acceptanceCriteria: Activity/tool/agent outputs used downstream are associated with the producing attempt identity.
Downstream input references resolve to a specific producing Step Execution output, not ambiguous latest, unless the workflow explicitly refreshes them.
A new accepted Step Execution that changes semantic outputs marks dependent downstream steps pending/blocked/superseded/requires_revalidation.

Preserved steps carry preservedFrom workflowId/runId/logicalStepId/executionOrdinal and are reused only when a checkpoint proves the output still satisfies downstream contracts; missing/unauthorized/corrupted/plan-mismatched preserved refs fail before new work.
Gate-based validation of downstream validity is recorded as structured evidence, not prose.
requirements: Bind outputs to producing attempt identity.
Invalidate/revalidate dependents on changed accepted outputs.
Define preservedFrom provenance and checkpoint-gated reuse.

sourceDesignCoverage.ownership: Owns dependency invalidation rules and preserved output reuse contracts.

risks: Required for both resume and autonomous loops; missing invalidation risks presenting stale downstream work as valid.

stories.description: As an operator, I need to recover a failed task by creating a linked follow-up execution that starts a new local Step Execution at the failed logical step from a validated checkpoint, preserving completed prior steps from refs, so that recovery never reconstructs progress from logs, never silently reruns or degrades to full rerun, and never reuses missing or inconsistent checkpoint evidence.
stories.whyItExists: Failed-step recovery is a primary consumer of the primitive and a high-value operator action; it composes checkpoints, lineage, and dependency invalidation.
stories.scope: Resume creating a linked follow-up execution with reason recover_from_failed_step; new local Step Execution at the failed step (local attempt usually 1) with pinned source workflowId/runId lineage; importing completed prior steps as preserved progress from refs; restoring the workspace from a validated checkpoint; executing the failed step first then later steps subject to dependency invalidation; the explicit resume must-not rules.
stories.outOfScope: The checkpoint contract/validation it relies on (STORY-004); dependency invalidation rules it applies (STORY-008); the lineage field contract (STORY-001).
stories.independentTest: Resume a run with steps 1-2 succeeded and step 3 failed and assert steps 1-2 are preserved from refs (not rerun), step 3 starts as a new local execution from a validated checkpoint with lineage pinned to the source run, and resume fails explicitly on missing/corrupted/unauthorized/inconsistent checkpoint evidence rather than degrading to full rerun.
acceptanceCriteria: Resume creates a linked follow-up execution and starts a new local Step Execution at the failed logical step rather than continuing the old step in place.

Resume attempt properties hold: reason recover_from_failed_step, local attempt scoped to the resumed run, lineage.sourceExecutionOrdinal/lineageExecutionOrdinal set, and source workflowId/runId pinned.
Completed prior steps are imported as preserved progress from refs and not re-executed unless a future explicit mode requests it.
The workspace is restored from a validated checkpoint and later steps execute normally after the failed step succeeds, subject to dependency invalidation.

Resume must not allow silent task input edits, must not silently fall back to full rerun, must not ignore missing/corrupted/unauthorized/inconsistent checkpoint evidence, and must not conflate local attempt identity with cross-run lineage identity.
requirements: Implement linked follow-up recovery execution semantics.
Preserve prior steps from refs and restore from validated checkpoint.
Enforce the resume must-not rules including no silent full-rerun fallback.

sourceDesignCoverage.ownership: Owns the Resume relationship, resume attempt properties, and resume must-not rules.

risks: Must not silently degrade to full rerun (core invariant 15); depends on checkpoint validation and dependency invalidation.

stories.summary: Managed runtime relationship and runtime context policy
stories.description: As a MoonMind workflow, I need to own Step Execution identity, checkpoint policy, durable evidence refs, and advancement while managed runtimes only execute attempts, and to record a runtime context policy distinct from workspace policy, so that agents can recommend but never unilaterally create hidden attempts or mutate the attempt ledger and clean-context reattempts are explicit.
stories.whyItExists: Runtime adapters execute work but must not own attempt semantics; clean-context reattempts and external-agent limits must be explicit.
stories.scope: The MoonMind.Run responsibilities for a managed agent runtime attempt (create record, record immutable context bundle, validate/apply workspace policy, launch/reuse AgentRun per runtime context policy, collect output refs, run/schedule gates, classify side effects, update ledger/manifest); runtime context policy distinct from workspace policy with suggested values (fresh_agent_run, reuse_session_new_epoch, reuse_session_same_epoch, external_provider_continuation); the rule that agents recommend but do not create hidden attempts or mutate the ledger; external/coordinated agents still record attempt identity/evidence where available.
stories.outOfScope: Workspace policy semantics (STORY-005); context bundle contract (STORY-003); gate execution (STORY-007).
stories.independentTest: Drive a managed-runtime attempt and assert MoonMind owns identity/checkpoint/advancement, the runtime context policy is recorded separately from the workspace policy, an agent recommendation does not create a new attempt or mutate the ledger, and an external-agent attempt still records attempt identity and available evidence.
acceptanceCriteria: For a managed agent runtime attempt, MoonMind.Run performs the documented orchestration responsibilities and owns Step Execution identity, checkpoint policy, durable evidence refs, and advancement decisions.
A runtime context policy is recorded and is distinct from the workspace policy, using the suggested values.
An agent may recommend another attempt but must not unilaterally create hidden attempts or mutate the attempt ledger.

Clean-context reattempts for managed sessions resolve to fresh_agent_run plus session reset semantics, while external/coordinated agents still record attempt identity, context refs, known side effects, and available checkpoint evidence.
requirements: Define MoonMind.Run managed-runtime orchestration responsibilities.
Define runtime context policy distinct from workspace policy.
Forbid agent-created hidden attempts and ledger mutation.

sourceDesignCoverage.ownership: Owns the managed runtime relationship and runtime context policy.

risks: External-provider runtimes may lack direct workspace control; features requiring restoration may need a different policy.

stories.summary: Activity surface and worker boundary contract
stories.description: As a MoonMind platform maintainer, I need orchestration owned by MoonMind.Run and all side effects owned by idempotent activities with compact typed boundary results, so that workflow code never performs IO/git/provider/memory operations directly and large outputs cross boundaries only as artifact refs.
stories.whyItExists: The architectural ownership boundary is a non-negotiable platform contract that all other Step Execution behavior must respect; it keeps workflow payloads compact and replay-safe.
stories.scope: MoonMind.Run owns orchestration, activities own side effects, runtime adapters execute but do not own attempt semantics; suggested activity families and task queues; the rules that workflow code must not read files/run git/inspect artifacts/call providers/mutate memory directly, that boundary results are compact and typed, that large outputs are artifact refs, and that activities are idempotent or guarded by attempt-derived keys.
stories.outOfScope: The specific contents produced by each activity family (manifests, checkpoints, gates, etc., owned by their respective stories); idempotency key derivation specifics (STORY-006).
stories.independentTest: Inspect the workflow/activity boundary and assert workflow code performs no direct IO/git/provider/memory operations, activity boundary results are compact typed payloads with large outputs passed as refs, and side-effecting activities are idempotent or key-guarded.
acceptanceCriteria: Workflow code does not read files, run git, inspect artifacts, call providers, or mutate memory directly.
Activity results crossing the workflow boundary are compact and typed, and large outputs are persisted as artifacts and passed back as refs.
Side-effecting activities are idempotent or guarded by idempotency keys derived from attempt identity.
Suggested activity families map to appropriate task queues without requiring every activity to exist in the first implementation slice.
requirements: Enforce orchestration/side-effect ownership separation.
Enforce compact typed boundary results and ref-passing for large outputs.
Require idempotent or key-guarded activities.

sourceDesignCoverage.ownership: Owns the activity surface, worker boundary rules, and queue families.

risks: Boundary violations would break replay safety and compatibility (coordinate with STORY-014).

stories.summary: Security and side-effect guardrails for Step Executions
stories.description: As a security-conscious platform, I need Step Execution context bundles, manifests, checkpoints, retrieval manifests, and memory proposals to exclude raw secrets, respect artifact authorization, scope workspace restores to approved workspaces without path traversal, and sanitize failed-attempt logs, so that the reattempt and recovery machinery preserves MoonMind's security posture.
stories.whyItExists: Reattempt/recovery surfaces touch credentials, artifacts, external systems, and the filesystem; the design's security rules must be explicitly owned.
stories.scope: No raw credentials in context bundles/manifests/checkpoints/retrieval/memory; artifact refs respect normal authorization; workspace restore scoped to the run workspace or approved worktree with no path traversal or writes outside approved workspaces; non-idempotent external work not reattempted without explicit policy; gate-approved state for publication/Jira/merge/deploy/provider-account; sanitized failed-attempt logs/diagnostics before display or publication; repo/local skill sources treated as untrusted input respecting skill source policy.
stories.outOfScope: The functional side-effect classification and gating (STORY-006/007, referenced here); the context bundle/checkpoint contracts themselves (STORY-003/004, whose credential-exclusion this story enforces as a security guarantee).
stories.independentTest: Scan generated bundles/manifests/checkpoints/retrieval/memory artifacts for secret patterns and assert none are present, assert artifact reads enforce authorization, assert a restore attempting path traversal or out-of-workspace writes is rejected, and assert failed-attempt diagnostics are sanitized before display/publication.
acceptanceCriteria: Context bundles, manifests, checkpoints, retrieval manifests, and memory proposals never contain raw credentials or secrets.
Artifact refs respect normal authorization checks.
Workspace restore operations are scoped to the run workspace or approved worktree and disallow path traversal or writes outside approved workspaces.
Non-idempotent external work is not reattempted without explicit policy and publication/Jira/merge/deploy/provider-account actions require gate-approved state.
Failed attempt logs and diagnostics are sanitized before display or publication and repo/local skill sources are treated as untrusted input respecting skill source policy.
requirements: Enforce secret exclusion across all Step Execution artifacts.
Enforce artifact authorization and scoped, traversal-safe restores.
Enforce log sanitization and untrusted skill-source handling.

sourceDesignCoverage.ownership: Owns the security and side-effect guardrail rules as enforceable guarantees.

risks: Security regressions here have outsized impact; overlaps with STORY-006 external guardrails.

stories.summary: Step Execution non-goals and scope guardrails
stories.description: As a MoonMind maintainer, I need the explicit non-goals enforced as negative guardrails across the Step Execution system, so that implementation does not drift into infinite loops, hidden single-transcript attempts, large payloads in workflow state, Temporal-retry-as-reattempt, automatic external replay, durable repo memory from failed attempts, separate resume/loop recovery systems, full attempt history in the default view, or forcing every Step Type into autonomous reattempt semantics.
stories.whyItExists: Non-goals must be explicitly owned so the coverage gate passes and so future work does not silently violate the design's stated exclusions.
stories.scope: Owning each stated non-goal as a negative acceptance guardrail and cross-referencing the stories that enforce the related positive behavior (loops/budgets STORY-007, payload-as-refs STORY-001/011, retry vs reattempt STORY-001, external replay STORY-006, failed-attempt memory STORY-003, unified recovery foundation STORY-009, default-view minimalism STORY-002).
stories.outOfScope: Implementing the positive behaviors themselves; this story only asserts the exclusions are not violated.
stories.independentTest: Review the implemented Step Execution behaviors against each non-goal and assert none are violated: bounded loops, no single-transcript attempt hiding, refs-only workflow state, distinct retry vs reattempt, no automatic full external replay, no durable repo memory from failed attempts, a single shared recovery foundation, latest-only default view, and no forced autonomous semantics for every Step Type.
acceptanceCriteria: Autonomous loops are bounded by budgets and never infinite (cross-checked with STORY-007).
Attempts are not hidden inside a single agent transcript and large diffs/logs are not stored in workflow state.
Temporal Activity retries are not treated as semantic reattempts.
External side effects are not automatically replayed and failed-attempt memory proposals are not committed directly to the repo.

Failed-step recovery and autonomous loops share one recovery foundation, the default task detail view does not expose full immutable attempt history, and no Step Type is forced into autonomous reattempt semantics.
requirements: Encode each non-goal as a negative guardrail.
Cross-reference enforcing stories for traceability.

sourceDesignCoverage.ownership: Owns the explicit non-goals as scope guardrails.

risks: Non-goals are cross-cutting; enforcement is distributed and must be verified against multiple stories.

stories.summary: In-flight Temporal compatibility and versioned cutover for Step Execution contracts
stories.description: As a platform maintainer rolling out Step Executions with Checkpointing, I need any new Step Execution or checkpoint contract that affects Temporal payloads to preserve in-flight compatibility or follow an explicit versioned cutover plan, so that runs already in flight continue safely as the phased rollout proceeds.
stories.whyItExists: Core invariant 17 and the phased rollout require that payload/contract changes never break in-flight workflows; this is a compatibility-sensitive Temporal contract.
stories.scope: Preserving worker-bound invocation compatibility for in-flight runs when manifest/checkpoint/context/gate payload shapes change, or defining an explicit versioned migration/cutover; honoring the phased rollout ordering (manifests/evidence, checkpoints, gated loops, retrieval/memory, autonomous loop) so each phase ships without breaking running workflows.
stories.outOfScope: The functional contracts themselves (owned by STORY-001/003/004/007); product UI history beyond compatibility concerns.
stories.independentTest: Introduce a Step Execution/checkpoint payload change and assert in-flight runs on the prior payload shape continue to execute correctly (compatibility test) or that an explicit versioned cutover path is exercised, covering unknown/blank/new field values per the repo's workflow-boundary testing rules.
acceptanceCriteria: New Step Execution and checkpoint contracts that affect Temporal payloads preserve in-flight compatibility or use an explicit versioned cutover plan.

A compatibility/replay-style regression test covers the previous payload/history shape for runs already in flight, or the change documents why in-flight compatibility is impossible and how cutover is made safe.
Phased rollout ordering is respected so each phase ships without breaking running workflows.
Degraded/unknown/blank/new status or field values are handled at the workflow boundary rather than crashing in-flight runs.
requirements: Preserve in-flight payload compatibility or define versioned cutover.
Add workflow-boundary compatibility/replay coverage for payload changes.
Sequence delivery per the phased rollout.

sourceDesignCoverage.ownership: Owns in-flight Temporal compatibility and the versioned cutover/phased rollout requirement.

risks: Applies to every payload-affecting story; must coordinate cutover across STORY-001/003/004/007.

notes: The task prompt referenced docs/Steps/StepAttemptsAndCheckpointing.md; the canonical file in the repository is docs/Steps/StepExecutionsAndCheckpointing.md (the design renames 'Attempt' to 'Step Execution'). The existing repo file was used as the source design and its path is preserved in every story's sourceReference.
